title: New Congress to convene, but will McCarthy be House speaker?
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new Congress opens with House Republican leader Kevin McCarthy grasping for his political survival, with the potential to become the first nominee for speaker in 100 years to fail to win initial support from his own colleagues in a high-stakes vote for the gavel.

title: Jan. 6 panel shutting down after referring Trump for crimes
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee is shutting down, having completed a whirlwind 18-month investigation of the 2021 Capitol insurrection and having sent its work to the Justice Department along with a recommendation for prosecuting former President Donald Trump.

title: 2024 contest comes into view with hurdles for both parties
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Both political parties are opening the new year confronting critical questions about the people and policies they want to embrace as the next election speeds into view.The challenges are particularly urgent for Republicans, who hoped to enter 2023 with a secure grip on one, if not both, chambers of Congress.

title: EXPLAINER: How the House of Representatives elects a speaker
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kevin McCarthy is set to face a case of deja vu come Tuesday. The political future of the 57-year-old will once again be at stake as Republican lawmakers decide if he should be elected as House speaker.

title: Democrat Katie Hobbs takes office as Arizona governor
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Katie Hobbs took the oath of office Monday to become Arizona’s 24th governor and the first Democrat to hold the office since 2009.Hobbs was sworn in during a private ceremony at the state Capitol as she formally took over from Republican Doug Ducey.

title: Kaptur, longtime Ohio Democrat: Congress overlooks heartland
shortDescription: TOLEDO, Ohio (AP) — When the new Congress convenes on Tuesday, Ohio Democratic Rep. Marcy Kaptur will become the longest-serving woman in its history. Yet after 40 years, she sometimes feels like an outsider.

title: Biden’s new year pitch focuses on benefits of bipartisanship
shortDescription: CHRISTIANSTED, U.S. Virgin Islands (AP) — President Joe Biden and top administration officials will open a new year of divided government by fanning out across the country to talk about how the economy is benefiting from his work with Democrats and Republicans.

title: Santos should consider resigning, veteran GOP lawmaker says
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Even as the House GOP leadership keeps silent, a veteran Republican lawmaker said Sunday that George Santos should consider resigning after the congressman-elect from New York admitted to lying about his heritage, education and professional career.

title: Trump rings in 2023 facing headwinds in his White House run
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump began 2022 on a high. Primary candidates were flocking to Florida to court the former president for a coveted endorsement.

title: South Korea: Talks with US on management of nukes underway
shortDescription: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea reconfirmed Tuesday that Seoul and Washington are discussing its involvement in U.S. nuclear asset management in the face of intensifying North Korean nuclear threats, after President Joe Biden denied that the allies were discussing joint nuclear exercises.

title: Mississippi legislators could debate tax cuts again in 2023
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — Mississippi legislators return to the Capitol on Tuesday, and their three-month session could be dominated by debates over taxes.

title: Officials must choose between jobs or office with new law
shortDescription: MIAMI (AP) — A new Florida law is forcing some public officials to choose between their day jobs or holding office.The measure, which took effect over the weekend, prohibits public officials from working as lobbyists while holding public office.

title: Vermont Legislature to focus on housing, workforce, climate
shortDescription: MONTPELIER, Vt. (AP) — The new Vermont Legislature, which is scheduled to begin work Wednesday, is expected to focus its attention on continuing to confront the state’s housing shortage, workforce development and climate change, said the Democratic speaker of the House of Representatives.

title: NY lawmakers get pay raise making them nation’s best-paid
shortDescription: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Just in time for the New Year, New York lawmakers have become the highest paid state legislators in the nation under a bill signed Saturday. Members of both houses are getting a pay raise of $32,000, for a base salary of $142,000, under a bill Gov.

title: Historic term begins in Michigan as Whitmer, others sworn in
shortDescription: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer was sworn in for second term as the state's 49th governor on Sunday, pushing a message of unity and working together during remarks on the state Capitol steps as Democrats took full control of the state government for the first time in 40 years.

title: Illinois high court halts elimination of cash bail
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Illinois Supreme Court has halted provisions of a new law that would eliminate cash bail for criminal defendants, issuing a stay hours before the new policies were set to take effect Sunday.

title: How a new Congress will change the direction of the country: What we know
shortDescription: House GOP Leader Kevin McCarthy said having Republicans in the majority will change the direction of the country. The new Congress is sworn in Jan. 3.

title: Culture wars: House Republicans attack Pentagon for 'woke' policies
shortDescription: House Republicans plan to open a new front in the culture wars: attacking the Pentagon for its "woke" social policies.

title: As GOP takes the House, a blockade for Biden's congressional agenda
shortDescription: President Joe Biden’s agenda will largely be blunted in a divided Congress, with GOP lawmakers pledging to block Democrats' legislative priorities.

title: Trump hosts New Year's Eve party, ending year with legal, political setbacks
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump had some legal and political notable setbacks in 2022. He ended it with his annual New Year's Eve party at Mar-a-Lago.

title: Week one of the new Congress: Picking a speaker, investigations, more
shortDescription: The 118th Congress is due to be seated on Tuesday and lawmakers have some housekeeping before they can get to business. Here's what to keep an eye on.

title: State AG: Ex-Trump chief of staff Meadows won’t face NC voter fraud charges
shortDescription: Mark Meadows, former White House chief of staff to Trump, won’t face voter fraud charges for his 2020 registration and absentee vote, N.C. AG said.

title: What happened this year in politics? Midterm elections, Roe overturned
shortDescription: In a busy year for politics, the midterm elections shared the stage with the Supreme Court, ex-President Trump's legal troubles and more during 2022.

title: OnPolitics special edition: Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Nearly 6,000 pages include personal returns, business returns.

title: Top takeaways from Donald Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: Six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns have been released by Congress after an eight-year legal battle.

title: Ex-President Donald Trump's tax returns released: What we know
shortDescription: The release contained six years of tax returns over the time Donald Trump was president and campaigned for the White House, from 2015 to 2020.

title: Biden is granting six pardons. Here's who they are
shortDescription: The White House said President Joe Biden granted clemency through a deliberative process in coordination with the Department of Justice.

title: Read the documents: Donald Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump's personal and business tax returns over six years include thousands of pages. Read a copy of Trump's tax returns here.

title: On tropical vacation, Biden mulls: To be or not to be (a 2024 candidate)?
shortDescription: President Joe Biden, on new year's holiday in St. Croix, says he plans to run for the White House but wants to talk a 2024 campaign over with family.

title: Biden signs $1.7 trillion bill funding government operations
shortDescription: President Joe Biden signed a $1.7 trillion spending bill to keep the federal government operating through federal budget year-end in September 2023.

title: Ex-Transportation secretary Elaine Chao addresses Trump's "racist taunt"
shortDescription: Chao and her husband, Republican Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell, typically stay mum about former President Donald Trump's inflammatory comments.

title: OnPolitics: 6 years of Trump's tax returns to be released Friday
shortDescription: Trump's tax returns from 2015-2020 are expected to be released Friday after a House panel vote. What to know

title: 118th Congress to be sworn in, both parties will have slim majorities with split control
shortDescription: Without full Democratic control in both chambers, the Biden administration could face some uphill battles, like Republican legislation and possible investigations.

title: Arizona Republican Kari Lake appeals election challenge loss
shortDescription: Failed GOP Arizona gubernatorial candidate Lake is appealing her election challenge loss and a decision ordering her to pay Katie Hobbs' legal fees.

title: Trump tax returns will be released Friday, House panel says
shortDescription: A U.S. House committee is planning to release six years of former President Donald Trump’s tax returns Friday, a spokesperson confirmed to USA TODAY.

title: SCOTUS blocks Biden's ending Title 42
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: Supreme Court blocks Biden from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: Biden Caps Two Years of Action on the Economy, With New Challenges Ahead
shortDescription: The president used a slim congressional majority to enact legislation that could reshape the American economy — if his administration can make the laws succeed.By Jim Tankersley

title: Here Are the House Republicans to Watch if McCarthy’s Bid for Speaker Falters
shortDescription: Representative Kevin McCarthy has so far faced no viable challenger for the speakership. But if he is unable to secure the votes, an alternative could quickly emerge.By Catie Edmondson

title: A Failed Speaker Vote for Kevin McCarthy Would Be a Historic Event
shortDescription: A small group of Republicans has promised to vote against Kevin McCarthy this week. They could throw the House into chaos not seen for a century.By Brendan Buck

title: Retiring Congress Members See Rough Roads Ahead. They Won’t Miss the Gridlock.
shortDescription: Departing longtime lawmakers perceive little prospect of orderly business in a divided Congress. They’re taking with them decades of experience in the day-to-day work of keeping the government running.By Emily Cochrane

title: Paul Pelosi, el marido que se ocupa de las tareas mundanas
shortDescription: La pareja de Nancy Pelosi fundó una firma de inversión en capital de riesgo, pero desde que la presidenta de la Cámara de Representantes optó por la política, es quien compra las toallas de cocina y el guardarropa de ella.By Annie Karni

title: The ‘Red Wave’ Washout: How Skewed Polls Fed a False Election Narrative
shortDescription: The errant surveys spooked some candidates into spending more money than necessary, and diverted help from others who otherwise had a fighting chance of winning.By Jim Rutenberg, Ken Bensinger and Steve Eder

title: McCarthy relents on key conservative demand — but uncertainty remains over speaker bid
shortDescription: The proposed tweak to the House’s no-confidence vote could present a serious threat to McCarthy as his conference takes power this week.
